src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.general_idea: Changed the return type of getBookDetails from the interface BookDetails to the implementation class BookDetailsImpl.
src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.why_exists: Compile error: method signature mismatch if callers expect the interface type or if the implementation class is not assignable in the expected context; also indicates a shift away from interface-based abstraction in this API.

src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.general_idea: Changed the generic type of the assortment map from the interface BookDetails to the concrete implementation BookDetailsImpl.
src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.why_exists: Compile error: The map is populated with BookDetailsImpl instances and methods return BookDetailsImpl, so the generic type must match the actual stored/returned type to avoid type mismatch or require casting.

src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.general_idea: Removed the overloaded constructor with a boolean populate flag and consolidated initialization into a single private constructor that always populates data.
src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.why_exists: Compile error: The public default constructor is removed. Any code outside the class attempting to instantiate AssortmentImpl directly will fail to compile.
src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.before: public AssortmentImpl() { this(true); } private AssortmentImpl(boolean populate) { ... if (populate) { populateWithDummyData(); } }
src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.after: private AssortmentImpl() { assortmentMap = new LinkedHashMap<Long, BookDetailsImpl>(); populateWithDummyData(); }

src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.general_idea: Changed the return type of getBookDetails from the interface BookDetails to the concrete implementation BookDetailsImpl.
src/main/java/eu/kartoffelquadrat/bookst.why_exists: Compile error: If the Assortment interface defines the return type as BookDetails, changing the implementation to return BookDetailsImpl is valid, but if the interface was also updated or if callers expect the interface type, this change tightens the contract. However, primarily this is a breaking change if the interface signature was also changed to match, or if it violates Liskov Substitution Principle in a strict typing context. Given the map change, this is likely part of a broader refactor to use concrete types.
